description_long: This is a classical-style marble sculpture depicting a nude male figure standing upright with his right arm raised and his left arm resting on a draped object, possibly a lion skin or a similar pelt. The figure is missing its head and right hand, and stands on a rectangular plinth. The anatomical details are finely rendered, with visible musculature and naturalistic proportions. Drapery hangs from the left arm and cascades down beside the figure, adding texture and contrast to the otherwise smooth surface. The sculpture has a weathered, slightly aged patina, suggesting it is modeled after or inspired by ancient Greco-Roman statuary.
description: A headless, nude male marble sculpture with a raised right arm and draped fabric over the left arm, standing on a rectangular base. The figure is detailed and classical in style, with a weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full frontal anatomy, raised right arm, and draped fabric over the left arm.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the figure, the draped fabric, and the muscular definition of the torso and legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back musculature, the draped fabric hanging down, and the figure's stance on the plinth.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the extended right leg, the raised arm, and the side of the draped fabric.
attribute_subject_matter: classical nude male figure with drapery
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
